instagram_viral_reels.summary: @ - 0 likes / 0 comments in 24h. #. Automation angle: Study the first-2s hook + caption format — replicate the structure for your AI-automation Instagram reels.

anthropic_claude_news.title: Securing Model Context Protocol: A Roadmap for Post-Quantum AI Infrastructure - Security Boulevard
anthropic_claude_news.summary: Security Boulevard lays out a roadmap for hardening Model Context Protocol deployments against future quantum-era threats, arguing MCP is becoming critical AI infrastructure that inherits every classical secrets-management problem plus new ones. Recommendations include short-lived credentials for MCP servers and post-quantum-ready transport. Automation angle: what to build — an MCP security-audit checklist is a portfolio piece almost no one has, and every company adopting Claude Code + MCP will need one.

anthropic_claude_news.title: Anthropic slashes Claude Fable 5 limits in Max and Team Premium and pushes Pro users toward API pricing
anthropic_claude_news.summary: Anthropic will bundle Claude Fable 5 into Max and Team Premium plans from July 20 while cutting its usage limits by half, nudging heavy users toward API pricing. The move formalizes Fable 5 as the flagship tier after weeks of temporary access extensions. Automation angle: cost math matters — for automation builders, metered API access to Fable 5 with caching can now undercut seat-based plans; re-run your token budgets before renewing.

anthropic_claude_news.title: Anthropic includes Fable 5 for top-tier subscriptions after weeks of extensions
anthropic_claude_news.summary: Economic Times confirms Max and Team Premium subscribers get Claude Fable 5 built into their plans indefinitely, ending the rolling trial-extension limbo. Pro users keep pay-as-you-go access via the API. Automation angle: this stabilizes the top Claude tier for client work — you can now quote Fable 5-powered deliverables without pricing-change risk.

anthropic_claude_news.title: Chinese AI model takes US tech industry by surprise with abilities rivalling Claude, ChatGPT
anthropic_claude_news.summary: Moonshot's new Kimi K3 model has stunned the US industry with capabilities early testers say rival Claude and ChatGPT, at open-weight prices. The Beijing startup's release is drawing DeepSeek-moment comparisons across the industry. Automation angle: competitive context for interviews — knowing when an open-weight model is 'good enough' for a client workload is exactly the judgment AI-automation roles hire for.

anthropic_claude_news.title: Meta, Anthropic in talks for potential $10 billion compute lease deal
anthropic_claude_news.summary: Meta and Anthropic are reportedly negotiating a compute lease worth around $10 billion, which would let Meta monetize surplus data-center capacity while giving Anthropic a new supply line beyond its existing cloud partners. It would be one of the largest AI infrastructure deals ever between two rivals. Automation angle: macro signal — frontier labs are compute-constrained, so expect continued API rate ceilings; design automations that degrade gracefully under throttling.

ai_business_automation.title: Striking Workers Bring Car Factory to a Screeching Halt Over Humanoid Robots
ai_business_automation.summary: Workers at a car factory walked out and halted production over the deployment of AI-driven humanoid robots on the line, one of the first strikes aimed squarely at embodied automation rather than software. The dispute centers on whether robot rollouts require negotiated staffing guarantees. Automation angle: risk to watch — automation deployments that skip worker buy-in are now operational risks; change-management is part of the AI-automation job.

ai_business_automation.title: The Pentagon's new AI playbook treats slow adoption as a bigger risk than imperfect alignment
ai_business_automation.summary: The Pentagon's new AI adoption playbook explicitly treats slow adoption as a bigger institutional risk than imperfect alignment, directing programs to field AI systems faster and iterate on safety in deployment. It's a sharp doctrine shift with ripple effects for every defense contractor's automation roadmap. Automation angle: sector signal — government and defense are moving from pilots to mandates; procurement-savvy AI-automation skills are about to be in demand.

quantum_ai_research.title: Quantum Machine Learning Assesses Post-Quantum Protocol Resilience - Quantum Zeitgeist
quantum_ai_research.summary: Researchers applied quantum machine learning to assess the resilience of post-quantum cryptographic protocols, using QML models to probe weaknesses classical analysis misses. It's a concrete case of AI methods and quantum computing reinforcing each other in security. Automation angle: watch post-quantum readiness — it's becoming a compliance checkbox that AI-security automations will need to report on.

quantum_ai_research.title: Nvidia advances Japan’s quantum and AI for science capabilities - GamesBeat
quantum_ai_research.summary: NVIDIA is deepening Japan's quantum-AI research capacity, pairing its accelerated-computing stack with national quantum programs for science workloads. The partnership treats AI supercomputing and quantum processors as one hybrid pipeline. Automation angle: macro signal — hybrid quantum-classical AI infrastructure is where national budgets are flowing; adjacent tooling (job schedulers, result pipelines) is greenfield.

quantum_ai_research.title: New quantum algorithm could unlock faster AI and scientific computing - Interesting Engineering
quantum_ai_research.summary: Interesting Engineering covers a new quantum algorithm that could speed up core linear-algebra operations underpinning AI and scientific computing, potentially cutting training and simulation costs if realized on near-term hardware. Automation angle: long-horizon signal — algorithmic quantum speedups for AI workloads would reset inference economics; worth one line in any AI-strategy deck you write.

quantum_ai_research.title: AI is accelerating quantum computing - could our data be vulnerable? - RNZ
quantum_ai_research.summary: RNZ examines how AI is accelerating quantum computing development — from ML-designed error-correction codes to AI-optimized qubit control — and asks whether the resulting cryptography timeline puts today's data at risk. Automation angle: risk to watch — 'harvest now, decrypt later' is a data-governance conversation your enterprise clients should already be having.

quantum_ai_research.title: Quantum computing - what is the thing that'll be even bigger and smarter than AI? - Metro.co.uk
quantum_ai_research.summary: Metro's explainer asks what comes after AI and lands on quantum-AI convergence: quantum hardware accelerating machine learning workloads and AI in turn taming quantum error correction. A readable primer on why the two fields are fusing. Automation angle: macro signal — 'quantum + AI' is entering mainstream vocabulary; a simple explainer video on it is timely channel content.

quantum_ai_research.title: Bloq Quantum Partners with MIUUL to Expand Quantum Machine Learning in Turkey - Quantum Computing Report
quantum_ai_research.summary: Bloq Quantum partnered with Turkish education platform MIUUL to expand quantum machine learning training, building a QML talent pipeline outside the usual US/EU hubs. Automation angle: the QML education market is nascent — course-style content teaching quantum-AI basics has little competition and growing search demand.

quantum_ai_research.title: Saving Lives with Quantum Computing and AI - Millersville University
quantum_ai_research.summary: Millersville University researchers outline how combining quantum computing with AI could accelerate drug discovery and emergency-response modeling — applications where classical AI compute hits scaling walls. Automation angle: keep a watchlist of quantum-AI healthcare pilots; they generate strong newsletter/content material and early consulting niches.

product_showcase_opportunities.summary: MeitY / IndiaAI international acceleration tracks placing Indian AI startups into global programs. Rolling cohorts.

product_showcase_opportunities.eligibility: Any early-stage startup with a founding team; pre-launch and idea-stage are welcome.
product_showcase_opportunities.benefits: ~$500K on the standard deal, a 3-month program, and the lifelong YC network + Demo Day.

product_showcase_opportunities.title: Antler Residency (AI)
product_showcase_opportunities.eligibility: Solo founders welcome — no company or co-founder needed.
product_showcase_opportunities.benefits: Pre-seed cash, a co-founder matching residency, and hands-on mentorship.
product_showcase_opportunities.summary: Day-zero residency for founders incl. AI; rolling cohorts across 30+ cities.

product_showcase_opportunities.title: Techstars AI Accelerators
product_showcase_opportunities.eligibility: Early-stage startups; apply to the city/track that fits your team.
product_showcase_opportunities.benefits: Seed investment, a 3-month mentor-driven program, and a global investor network.
product_showcase_opportunities.summary: Multiple AI-focused Techstars programs; rolling applications by location.

product_showcase_opportunities.title: NVIDIA Inception
product_showcase_opportunities.eligibility: Any AI/ML startup at any stage — free to join.
product_showcase_opportunities.benefits: GPU and cloud credits, go-to-market support, and introductions to VCs.
product_showcase_opportunities.summary: Free program for AI/ML startups: GPU credits, go-to-market, VC intros. Rolling.

product_showcase_opportunities.title: Google for Startups AI Accelerator
product_showcase_opportunities.eligibility: Seed-to-Series-A AI startups; regional tracks including India.
product_showcase_opportunities.benefits: An equity-free program, Google mentorship, and cloud credits.
product_showcase_opportunities.summary: Equity-free AI accelerator with seasonal cohorts and regional tracks (incl. India).

product_showcase_opportunities.title: Google Selects 20 AI-First Indian Startups For Accelerator India 2026 Cohort - INDIA New England News
product_showcase_opportunities.summary: Google Selects 20 AI-First Indian Startups For Accelerator India 2026 Cohort - INDIA New England News

product_showcase_opportunities.title: Apply Now: Google Accelerator Support for AI Startups in India - ICTworks
product_showcase_opportunities.summary: Apply Now: Google Accelerator Support for AI Startups in India - ICTworks

elon_musk_ai_vision.title: Grok AI Review 2026: Features, Pricing & Honest Verdict - Memeburn
elon_musk_ai_vision.summary: Memeburn's hands-on review of Grok AI covers features, pricing and where it genuinely competes: strong real-time X data integration and coding value, weaker safety polish than rivals. Automation angle: competitive context for interviews — name-drop concrete Grok strengths/weaknesses to show you evaluate AI models on evidence, not brand.

elon_musk_ai_vision.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX in talks to supply the Pentagon with computing power: Report
elon_musk_ai_vision.summary: SpaceX employees have reportedly discussed supplying the Pentagon with computing power, positioning the company against neocloud providers for defense AI workloads. It's another thread in Musk's strategy of vertically integrating compute, models (Grok) and distribution across his companies. Automation angle: defense AI budgets are opening to non-traditional vendors — compliance-heavy automation experience is a differentiator worth building.

elon_musk_ai_vision.title: Grok 4.6 Confirmed: What We Know From the xAI Roadmap - BASENOR - Tesla Accessories
elon_musk_ai_vision.summary: Tesla-ecosystem outlet BASENOR rounds up what xAI's public roadmap shows about Grok 4.6: an incremental AI model release focused on coding and agentic reliability ahead of the 2T-parameter successor. Automation angle: competitive context — track Grok's agent tooling against Claude Code so you can speak to both stacks in interviews.

elon_musk_ai_vision.title: xAI's 2T Model: What Musk Just Revealed About Grok's Successor - BASENOR - Tesla Accessories
elon_musk_ai_vision.summary: A companion piece parses Musk's statements about the 2-trillion-parameter Grok successor: better than the current 1.5T model across the board, with initial training wrapping within days. Musk continues to frame xAI's AI trajectory as the fastest-accelerating in the industry. Automation angle: macro signal — frontier release cadence is now quarterly; build automations behind an abstraction layer so model swaps are config changes, not rewrites.

elon_musk_ai_vision.title: Our 2T model, which is better than our 1.5T in every way, will finish initial training next week. It might be able to exceed Kimi, but with speed and token efficiency close to our 1.5T (aka Grok 4.5). - x.com
elon_musk_ai_vision.source: Elon Musk on X (via Google News)
elon_musk_ai_vision.summary: Elon Musk says xAI's 2-trillion-parameter Grok successor — 'better than our 1.5T in every way' — finishes initial training next week. The post signals xAI is racing to ship its next frontier AI model on a compressed schedule against OpenAI and Anthropic. Automation angle: watch for Grok API pricing at launch — xAI has used aggressive pricing to buy share, which can cut inference costs for high-volume automations.

unaddressed_ai_problems.title: Data Centers Are Making Electricity Brutally Expensive for the Public
unaddressed_ai_problems.summary: AI data-center demand is driving double-digit electricity price increases for ordinary ratepayers across several US grids, and no regulator, utility or AI company currently owns the problem. Affected: households and small businesses subsidizing hyperscaler load growth; why it's open — costs are socialized through opaque rate cases while the demand driver (AI compute) sits outside utility regulation. Automation angle: unsolved gap — tools that attribute and forecast AI-driven grid cost impact for regulators basically don't exist yet.

unaddressed_ai_problems.title: Meta Patents Technology to Detect Your Mood by Constantly Analyzing Your Tone of Voice
unaddressed_ai_problems.summary: Meta has patented always-on AI mood detection from users' tone of voice, and there is no consent framework, disclosure standard or opt-out regime anywhere that covers passive emotional profiling. Affected: anyone using voice features on Meta platforms; why it's open — emotion inference falls between privacy law (which covers data) and consumer protection (which covers claims). Automation angle: risk to watch — emotional-AI compliance is a coming niche with zero incumbent tooling.

unaddressed_ai_problems.title: Top Meta Exec Describes Controversial Facial Recognition Feature in Detail After the Company Claimed It Didn’t Exist
unaddressed_ai_problems.summary: A top Meta executive described in detail a facial-recognition feature the company had previously claimed didn't exist, highlighting that there's still no mechanism forcing platforms to disclose which AI capabilities are actually running on user data. Affected: billions of users who can't verify what AI processing they're subject to; why it's open — audit rights for deployed AI features exist in no major jurisdiction. Automation angle: gap — independent AI-feature auditing is an unbuilt service category.

ai_business_opportunities.title: China's DeepSeek to raise fresh capital at $74 billion valuation ahead of onshore IPO
ai_business_opportunities.summary: DeepSeek is raising fresh capital at a $74 billion valuation ahead of an onshore IPO, weeks after its latest model release. The signal: capital markets now price efficient-training AI labs at frontier multiples. Automation angle: startable idea — 'DeepSeek-stack' automation consulting for cost-sensitive SMBs (open-weight models + n8n orchestration) sells the same efficiency story at service scale; why now — clients are actively asking for cheaper-than-OpenAI builds.

ai_business_opportunities.title: Neil Rimer thinks the AI money is coming back out
ai_business_opportunities.summary: Index Ventures co-founder Neil Rimer predicts the historic wealth AI is generating will be redistributed 'voluntarily or involuntarily' — and that the money is starting to come back out of AI concentration into applications. Automation angle: startable idea — application-layer AI services (vertical agents for clinics, logistics, local government) are where recycled AI capital is heading; for whom — non-technical SMB owners; why now — VCs are explicitly rotating from infra to applied AI.

global_ai_news.title: Family Horrified When They’re Forced to Sell Their Beloved Home to Make Way for AI Data Centers
global_ai_news.summary: A Georgia family says they were forced to sell their home of decades after Georgia Power moved to take the land for transmission infrastructure serving new AI data centers. The case has become a flashpoint for how the AI build-out is displacing residents who lack the resources to fight utility land acquisition. Automation angle: risk to watch — community backlash is becoming a real siting constraint for AI infrastructure, and it will shape where compute (and compute-adjacent jobs) can actually be built.

global_ai_news.title: US data center protests go national as backlash grows
global_ai_news.summary: Opponents of AI data center expansion are coordinating nationwide US protests this weekend under the HumansFirst banner, taking a previously local fight national. Organizers cite electricity prices, water use and land seizures as the movement's rallying points. Automation angle: macro signal — the social license for AI compute is eroding, which favors efficiency-focused automation work (smaller models, edge inference) over brute-force scaling.

global_ai_news.title: China's new World Artificial Intelligence Cooperation Organization is President Xi's clearest play yet for a parallel AI order
global_ai_news.summary: At the World AI Conference in Shanghai, Xi Jinping unveiled the World Artificial Intelligence Cooperation Organization, a China-led body pitched as an alternative to US-dominated AI governance. Analysts read it as Beijing's clearest bid yet to set global AI rules and export its stack to the Global South. Automation angle: watch which AI standards and model ecosystems emerging markets adopt — it determines which platforms your automation skills should target for international clients.

global_ai_news.title: Your Period Tracker Is (Probably) Spying on You
global_ai_news.summary: Wired's security roundup reports that popular period-tracking apps are still sharing sensitive reproductive-health data, alongside news of a breach exposing an AI mental-health platform's user records. The through-line: AI-adjacent consumer apps keep collecting far more than users realize. Automation angle: what to build — privacy-first AI wrappers and data-minimizing automations are a genuine differentiator as consumer trust craters.

global_ai_news.title: Open-weight models now match frontier cyber performance from just four months ago at a fraction of the cost
global_ai_news.summary: The British AI Security Institute finds open-weight models like GLM and Kimi now match the cyber-offense capabilities frontier closed models had just four months ago, at a fraction of the cost. The capability lag between open and closed models on security-relevant tasks has collapsed to roughly one product cycle. Automation angle: risk to watch — cheap open-weight capability means client security reviews of AI automations are about to get much stricter; bake in guardrails now.

global_ai_news.title: How Google’s New Gemini Rates Work and How to Track Your Usage
global_ai_news.summary: Wired breaks down Google's new Gemini usage-quota system, which now meters AI responses across free and paid tiers in ways many users won't notice until they hit limits. The piece explains how to track consumption and what actually counts against the caps. Automation angle: what workflow this unlocks — quota-aware routing (falling back between Gemini, Claude and open models) is a small automation that saves real money for AI-heavy teams.

global_ai_news.title: Prompt Injection Attacks Are Thwarting AI Hacking Agents
global_ai_news.summary: Defenders are successfully using 'context bombing' — flooding a malicious AI agent's context with instructions that trigger its own safety stops — to shut down AI-powered hacking agents mid-attack. It's one of the first documented cases of prompt injection being weaponized defensively at scale. Automation angle: what to try — the same context-hygiene techniques that stop attacks (input isolation, instruction pinning) are exactly what make client-facing agent automations safe to ship.

indian_ai_industry.title: Governance is the new moat in AI era: ServiceNow Top executive
indian_ai_industry.summary: ServiceNow's India MD Kulmeet Bawa says the company's next growth phase in India is becoming the enterprise 'AI control tower' — orchestrating AI workflows across systems rather than competing with foundation-model providers, with governance as the moat. Automation angle: this is the exact pitch of an AI-automation career — orchestration and governance across tools, not model building; use this framing in applications to Indian enterprise-AI teams.

ai_self_improvement_rsi.title: GPT-Red: Unlocking Self-Improvement for Robustness
ai_self_improvement_rsi.summary: OpenAI unveiled GPT-Red, a model trained to attack and probe OpenAI's own systems so its findings can be fed back into hardening future models — an explicit self-improvement loop for robustness. It's one of the clearest production examples of AI improving AI. Automation angle: the pattern (agent finds weaknesses → pipeline retrains) is reusable at small scale for evaluating your own automation agents.

ai_self_improvement_rsi.title: Meet GPT-Red: an LLM super-hacker OpenAI built to make its models safer
ai_self_improvement_rsi.summary: MIT Technology Review profiles GPT-Red, the 'LLM super-hacker' OpenAI built to make its models safer: an adversarial AI whose discoveries directly drive defensive training of successor models. The piece explores what happens when the red team is itself a frontier model. Automation angle: adversarial self-testing is coming to agent frameworks — expect 'red-team your agent' tooling to be a fundable niche.

ai_self_improvement_rsi.title: Exclusive: As Self-Evolving AI Surges, Humanity's Last Moat Is "Judgment," Says Tian Yuandong - finance.biggo.com
ai_self_improvement_rsi.summary: Meta researcher Tian Yuandong argues that as self-evolving AI systems surge, human judgment becomes the last durable moat — models can now improve their own components faster than humans can review the changes. Automation angle: position yourself as the judgment layer — AI-automation roles increasingly mean deciding what self-improving systems should optimize, not writing the code.

ai_self_improvement_rsi.title: Why Self-Evolving AI Cannot Replace Human Judgment Under the Current Technical Architecture - 36Kr
ai_self_improvement_rsi.summary: 36Kr's technical analysis argues self-evolving AI under current architectures still cannot replace human judgment: self-improvement loops optimize measurable proxies and drift from unmeasured intent. A grounded counterweight to RSI hype. Automation angle: risk to watch — automation systems that self-tune against metrics need human-defined guardrail metrics, a concrete deliverable you can sell.

ai_self_improvement_rsi.title: The Industrialization of Research ; On AI-Driven Science and Its Consequences
ai_self_improvement_rsi.summary: An arXiv position paper examines the 'industrialization of research' — AI systems now conducting substantial parts of the scientific pipeline — and its consequences for verification, credit and error propagation when AI-driven science outpaces human review. Automation angle: AI-conducted research pipelines need audit trails; provenance tooling for AI-generated findings is an open niche.

ai_self_improvement_rsi.title: AutoSynthesis: An agentic system for automated meta-analysis
ai_self_improvement_rsi.summary: AutoSynthesis, a new arXiv system, performs fully automated meta-analysis: agents retrieve studies, extract effect sizes and synthesize conclusions with minimal human input — AI doing the research-synthesis work of a review team. Automation angle: what to build — the same agentic retrieve-extract-synthesize loop applies to market research and competitive analysis for clients today.

ai_self_improvement_rsi.title: How to build self-improving AI-native companies - NextBigWhat
ai_self_improvement_rsi.summary: NextBigWhat distills how founders are building 'self-improving AI-native companies' — organizations whose core loops (support, product iteration, marketing) are agents that learn from their own outcomes. Automation angle: the playbook maps directly onto solo AI-automation businesses: instrument every workflow so the agent improves from its own run history.

ai_model_benchmarks.title: Text / LLM - Best: Claude Fable 5 / Runner-up: GPT-5.6 Sol
ai_model_benchmarks.summary: Current category leader: Claude Fable 5; runner-up GPT-5.6 Sol. Automation angle: switch your portfolio demos to whichever AI model ranks best in the category you build in this week.

ai_model_benchmarks.title: Coding - Best: Claude Fable 5 (via Claude Code) / Runner-up: Grok 4.5

ai_model_benchmarks.title: Kimi K3, and what we can still learn from the pelican benchmark
ai_model_benchmarks.summary: Simon Willison benchmarks Moonshot's newly announced Kimi K3 — 'their most capable model to date' — with his pelican-on-a-bicycle SVG test, finding it competitive with frontier Western AI models on generative reasoning. His takeaway: informal, hard-to-game benchmarks still surface real capability differences the leaderboards miss. Automation angle: build your own 3-prompt eval suite for client model selection — it's cheap, defensible, and a great portfolio artifact.

ai_model_benchmarks.title: Kimi's open model K3 nears GPT-5.6 Sol and Fable 5 while signaling the end of super cheap Chinese AI
ai_model_benchmarks.summary: The Decoder reports Kimi K3 — a 2.8-trillion-parameter multimodal open-weight AI model — lands within striking distance of GPT-5.6 Sol and Claude Fable 5 on public benchmarks, while its pricing signals the end of ultra-cheap Chinese inference. Automation angle: benchmark-watch — when open-weight models close within a few points of frontier, the automation-cost equation flips; re-quote your heavy-inference workflows.

new_ai_tools.title: Claude make Fable 5 permanent
new_ai_tools.summary: Simon Willison flags Anthropic's announcement that Claude Fable 5 becomes a permanent inclusion in Max and Team Premium plans from July 20. For Claude Code users this ends the uncertainty around whether the top AI model stays available for daily agentic coding work. Automation angle: what to try — pin Claude Code to Fable 5 for architecture/planning passes and drop to cheaper models for mechanical edits; the plan change makes that split sustainable.

new_ai_tools.title: Grok Build improves almost every day - x.com
new_ai_tools.source: Elon Musk on X (via Google News)
new_ai_tools.summary: Musk says Grok Build — xAI's recently open-sourced AI coding agent (Rust harness, TUI and tool layer) — is improving 'almost every day'. The open-source release makes it the most inspectable frontier-lab coding CLI, and a direct Claude Code competitor. Automation angle: what to try — read the Grok Build source to see how a production agent harness structures tools and context; it's a free masterclass for building your own agents.

new_ai_tools.title: Vertu wants executives to pay $6,880 for an AI agent — here’s how it actually performs
new_ai_tools.summary: TechCrunch reviews Vertu's $6,880 luxury foldable that ships with a built-in AI agent aimed at executives, testing whether the concierge-style agent justifies the price (mostly: no). Automation angle: market gap — 'AI agent as premium service' is being attempted top-down; a solo builder can undercut this with an n8n + Claude concierge workflow at 1% of the price.

general_news.title: US strikes hit Iran for seventh consecutive night
general_news.summary: US strikes hit Iran for a seventh consecutive night as the conflict grinds on. Iran retaliated against US allies in the region, with Kuwait reporting a power and water plant was hit.

general_news.title: Trump threatens new Canada tariffs over fires sending 'filthy' air into US cities
general_news.summary: Trump threatened new tariffs on Canada over wildfire smoke drifting into US cities, which he called 'filthy' air. Canadian leader Mark Carney countered that both countries share equal responsibility for fighting climate-driven fires.

general_news.title: Russian online retail warehouses hit by deadly Ukrainian strikes
general_news.summary: Deadly Ukrainian strikes hit Russian online-retail warehouses, extending Kyiv's campaign against economic targets deep inside Russia.

general_news.title: Sonam Wangchuk hunger strike LIVE: Wangchuk continues his fast in hospital; CJP's Dipke claims Delhi Police planning overnight 'crackdown' on protest
general_news.summary: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk continued his hunger strike from hospital after being forcibly admitted on day 20, as supporters allege police are planning an overnight crackdown on the protest site and at least 21 others fast alongside him.

general_news.title: Space start-up launches India’s first commercial rocket designed for orbit
general_news.summary: Skyroot Aerospace's Vikram-1 became India's first privately built orbital rocket to reach space, placing tech payloads into orbit and marking a milestone for the country's commercial space industry.

general_news.title: Helicopters dump water as huge Norway fire destroys homes
general_news.summary: A huge fire in Norway destroyed homes as helicopters dumped water on the blaze, one of the country's most destructive in years.

general_news.title: Laos says it can't determine cause of  tourist deaths linked to methanol
general_news.summary: Laos said it cannot determine the cause of tourist deaths previously linked to methanol poisoning, frustrating victims' families seeking accountability.
